entries.d: a very big town
entries.s: London is a very big _____ with millions of people, tall skyscrapers, and many underground trains.

entries.d: a green place in a town with trees
entries.s: Let's play football on the grass in the _____ after school, near the slides and swings.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with PARK?

entries.d: a road in a town with houses
entries.s: There are many shops selling clothes and food in our busy _____ in the town centre.

entries.d: a place where people buy and sell things
entries.s: We buy fresh fruit and vegetables from the farmer at the open-air _____ every Saturday morning.

entries.d: a big shop where you buy food
entries.s: Mum drives to the big _____ to push a trolley and buy food for the whole week.

entries.d: water that moves through the land
entries.s: We go swimming in the clear _____ in summer. The water flows between the two green banks.

entries.d: a place with many trees
entries.s: We go for a quiet walk in the _____ on Sunday afternoons, among the tall trees and singing birds.

entries.d: to leap into the water
entries.s: It is boiling hot outside! Let's take off our shoes and _____ and swim in the cold water.

entries.d: to see outside through the glass
entries.s: I _____ from my bedroom and see that it is raining cats and dogs outside.

entries.d: to take something from the ground
entries.s: There is a shiny coin on the floor next to your shoe. Can you bend down and _____?

entries.d: to be up in a tree, not moving
entries.s: The songbird likes to _____ on a high branch and sing to its friends.

entries.w: to fall out of the tree
entries.d: to drop down from a tree
entries.s: Be careful on the thin branch up there! You might slip and _____ onto the hard ground!

entries.d: to walk into a tree by mistake
entries.s: He was looking at his phone and not watching the path, so he _____ in the park and hurt his nose.

entries.d: to walk to the green place in town
entries.s: Let's _____ in the sunshine and play on the swings and slides this afternoon.

entries.d: to move something towards you
entries.s: The heavy wooden door is stuck shut. You must _____ the handle very hard towards you to open it.

entries.d: to go up a tree using hands and feet
entries.s: My pet cat likes to _____ and look down at the birds sitting on the branches.

entries.d: to go out of the work room
entries.s: Dad _____ at exactly five o'clock every day when his work is finished, and comes home for dinner.

entries.d: to see yourself in the glass
entries.s: She _____ every morning to check her hair and brush her teeth before going to school.

entries.d: to go up using your hands and feet
entries.s: The monkey children love to _____ up the big rocks in the playground like little mountain goats.

entries.d: to push yourself up into the air
entries.s: The small green frog can _____ very high into the air with its long strong back legs.

entries.d: to go away from a place
entries.s: We _____ the house at eight o'clock every morning to catch the school bus.

entries.d: a glass where you can see yourself
entries.s: There is a big round _____ on the bathroom wall. I can see my face in it when I brush my teeth.

entries.d: to place clothes on your body
entries.s: It is cold and windy outside. _____ your warm winter jacket before you go out!

entries.d: to or at another place, not here
entries.s: The scared bird flew _____ from the garden when my pet cat came running towards it.

entries.d: better than all others
entries.s: She is the _____ student in our whole class. She always gets the top mark and helps others.

entries.d: a person who finds out about crimes
entries.s: The police _____ wears a long coat and looks for clues at the crime scene to solve the case.

entries.d: what you call out when you need someone right now
entries.s: I can't carry these heavy boxes alone. _____ Please come and take some!

entries.d: a room where people work
entries.s: My mum works from 9 to 5 in an _____ in the city centre. She has a desk and a computer.

entries.d: to move very fast on your feet
entries.s: We _____ very fast to school because we are ten minutes late and the teacher is waiting.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with RUN?

entries.d: to get hold of something moving
entries.s: Throw the ball to me and I will _____ it in my hands without letting it fall!

entries.d: good at thinking and learning
entries.s: She always knows the answer to the hardest questions. She is very _____ — even cleverer than the teacher sometimes.

entries.d: to arrive at a place
entries.s: Walk down the long main street for five minutes and you _____ a big green park on your right.

entries.d: to have your home in a place
entries.s: I _____ in a small house near the school with my mum, dad, and little brother.

entries.d: good, pleasant, friendly
entries.s: Your new summer dress is very _____ — I love the flower pattern and the soft material.

entries.d: many, a big number of
entries.s: There are _____ children — about 300 — at our big school.

entries.d: to phone someone or say their name
entries.s: I will _____ you on the phone right after school and we can talk about the party.

entries.d: telling someone to hurry
entries.s: _____ Hurry up! We are five minutes late for the school bus and it is leaving now!

entries.d: to find the answer to a problem
entries.s: Can you _____ this tricky maths question? The answer is hidden and I cannot work it out.

entries.d: to stay and not go yet
entries.s: Please _____ here at the bus stop for two minutes. I will be back very soon with the tickets.

entries.d: to look at something for a time
entries.s: We like to _____ the colourful birds flying around the feeder in the garden through the window.

entries.d: to stand up or wake up from bed
entries.s: I _____ out of bed at seven o'clock every morning when my alarm clock rings.

entries.d: to change into something new
entries.s: When I finish university, I want to _____ a doctor who helps sick children in the hospital.

entries.d: saying something is really real
entries.s: You don't believe my crazy story about the flying elephant? _____ I saw it with my own eyes!

entries.d: telling someone to continue
entries.s: That's a very interesting story about your adventure. _____ Tell me what happened next!

entries.d: saying someone did a good job
entries.s: You got all ten questions right on the test! _____ I am very proud of you.
